user_message: I need to pull the context for the Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery engagement. There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 14:40 EST
Subject: Re: Re: whitespace analysis Timeline

Just checking in on the whitespace analysis proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-22 17:51 EST
Subject: Follow-up: whitespace analysis Review

There's been a change in our timeline for whitespace analysis. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 08:35 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with whitespace analysis. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-02-23
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on whitespace analysis from our last session.

[00:07:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:11:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:19:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:24:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:26:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:30:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:33:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

content.title: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery - whitespace analysis

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
